chief_complaint: A 56-year-old Chinese female patient presented with recurrent acute necrotizing pancreatitis lasting for 4 weeks.
history: The patient had a history of hypercalcemia-associated acute pancreatitis 3 years earlier. She presented with hypercalcemic crisis (serum calcium 4.49 mmol/L) and markedly elevated parathyroid hormone (2491 pg/mL). A left parathyroid adenoma was identified, implying primary hyperparathyroidism.
exam: On admission, she was afebrile with normal inflammatory markers.
labs: Computed tomography revealed walled-off pancreatic necrosis without signs of infection. Laboratory tests showed a hypercalcemic crisis (serum calcium 4.49 mmol/L) and markedly elevated parathyroid hormone (2491 pg/mL). Neck computed tomography and ultrasound identified a left parathyroid adenoma (33 x 21 x 23 mm).
